title: Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Frederick II was King of Sicily from 1198, King of Germany from 1212, King of Italy and Holy Roman Emperor from 1220, and King of Jerusalem from 1225 to 1228.

title: Baldwin I of Jerusalem becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Baldwin I was the first count of Edessa from 1098 to 1100 and king of Jerusalem from 1100 to his death in 1118.

title: Conradin becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Conrad III, called the Younger or the Boy, but usually known by the diminutive Conradin, was the last direct heir of the House of Hohenstaufen.

title: Conrad of Montferrat becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Conrad of Montferrat was a nobleman, one of the major participants in the Third Crusade.

title: Henry II of Jerusalem becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Henry II was the last crowned King of Jerusalem and also ruled as King of Cyprus.

title: Henry II, Count of Champagne becomes King of Jerusalem
fact: Henry II was the count of Champagne from 1181 and the lord of the Kingdom of Jerusalem from his marriage to Isabella I of Jerusalem in 1192 until his death in 1197.
